evidence_quotes.quote: the arbitrary shutdown of online video games and digital services by corporations, even when consumers have paid for access or ownership.

paragraphs.text: Dear Commissioners, I am writing to you as a concerned EU citizen to strongly urge the European Commission to include the objectives of the European Citizens Initiative "Stop Killing Games" within the framework of the upcoming Digital Fairness Act. This initiative raises urgent and legitimate concerns regarding consumer rights, digital ownership, and cultural preservation in the European digital market. The "Stop Killing Games" initiative addresses a growing problem: the arbitrary shutdown of online video games and digital services by corporations, even when consumers have paid for access or ownership. This practice undermines fundamental consumer rights and contradicts the principles of digital fairness, transparency, and sustainability that the EU seeks to promote. Millions of Europeans, particularly younger generations, engage in digital gaming as both entertainment and creative expression. The sudden and permanent removal of these gameswithout alternatives, compensation, or preservationamounts to a form of cultural erasure and commercial injustice. This is not merely a consumer issue; it is a question of digital heritage, market fairness, and citizens trust in the digital single market. We believe the goals of "Stop Killing Games" align with the core aims of the Digital Fairness Act: 1. Ensuring fairness in digital transactions by protecting consumers from arbitrary service terminations. 2. Guaranteeing transparency in digital product lifespans and end-of-life policies. 3. Preserving digital culture as an integral part of European cultural policy. I respectfully request that the Commission treat this initiative as a serious public mandate and integrate its objectives into the Digital Fairness Act. European consumers deserve a digital environment where ownership means permanence, and where digital goods are treated with the same respect as physical products. Thank you for your attention to this matter. I trust that you will consider the voices of European citizens and act in defense of a truly fair and future-proof digital Europe. Yours faithfully, Marlon B.
